dry_bulk.confluence_note: Significant spot rate expansion and a 99.6% 5-year BDI percentile contrast sharply with persistent negative fundamental sentiment and weak Chinese industrial data.
dry_bulk.summary: The dry bulk market is in a technical expansion regime with Capesize and Supramax classes showing high Z-scores, yet the underlying Chinese steel sector remains weak with production down 3.6% YOY and iron ore inventories ballooning by 20.3%. High spot rates appear increasingly decoupled from commodity demand, potentially buoyed by high bunker costs and geopolitical premiums.
dry_bulk.outlook: Cautiously bearish in the medium term as high inventories and low steel production may eventually trigger a correction in spot rates.
dry_bulk.watch: Chinese steel production YTD trends and iron ore portside inventory drawdown rates.

tanker.confluence_note: Extreme quantitative signals, including a Clean Tanker Z-score of +3.21, are backed by positive fundamental outlooks linked to unnavigable straits and disrupted supply chains.
tanker.summary: Tanker markets are witnessing an explosive expansion with Clean and Dirty rates showing ROC60 of 147% and 75% respectively as the market adjusts to unnavigable straits. While short-term momentum has softened, the structural shift toward longer routes and elevated oil prices maintains a strong floor for VLCC and Suezmax rates.
key_signals: Global Crude Oil On Water volatility
tanker.outlook: Highly bullish as geopolitical constraints on maritime straits continue to tighten global vessel supply.
tanker.watch: Navigability status of Middle Eastern straits and OECD crude oil inventory draws.

macro_note: A stark divide exists between the energy and industrial sectors; tankers are thriving on geopolitical disruption and supply-side constraints, while dry bulk faces a growing disconnect between high freight rates and cooling Chinese industrial demand.
